original text:
As the lengthy synopsis goes, season two of American Gods will see the battle between Old Gods and New Gods continues to brew as we join Mr. Wednesday just a few short hours after his declaration of war and the epic showdown that ensued at Easter's party.
While Mr. World plans revenge for Wednesday's attack, Mr. Wednesday continues his quest to pitch the case for war to the Old Gods with Shadow, Laura and Mad Sweeney in tow. When things don't go as planned after an encounter at the House on the Rock, both Old and New Gods, as well as those they meet along the way, find themselves on quests across America — all destined in some way for Cairo, Illinois.
Amazon Prime Video UK, who air the show in the UK, have confirmed that American Gods season two will be available every Monday, less than 24 hours after it airs on Starz in the US.
The first episode will air on Monday 11th March 2019.

SSL Certificates: the smart way to protect information.
If you are toying with the idea of starting your own business website, you probably came across mentions of SSL Certificates and you might be wondering...what is that?
SSL Certificates are actually also known as digital certificates, and their purpose is to allow a browser (typically, a person's computer or device) to a particular server (wherever the website is hosted online. Best Domain Registrar Australia 2018 can quickly patch the latest vulnerabilities & security threats.
SSL certificates enable users to protect their privacy, as well as sensitive information, such as any data you might type into a website. Our Melbourne team is incredibly dedicated to providing high availability to assist you with Email Hosting Services in Australia.
This is particularly important for Australia eCommerce websites, which handle various personal data on a daily basis, including addresses, phone numbers, credit card information and much more.
If you are looking to secure your websites, learn more about our SSL Certificates!

original text:
You would be most welcome!
At St Marks roundabout take the exit onto Wain A Long Road (signposted Laverstock). Proceed down this road (there are lots of parked cars and you may have to give way to oncoming traffic). At the bottom of the road turn right. At the mini round about turn right up the hill past Godolphin Prep. The entrance for The Godolphin School is on your left at the top of the hill. There is plenty of parking on site.
Walk up Milford Street, passing under the Ring Road, and right up to the top of Milford Hill. Godolphin School is just on the other side of the mini round about.
Love God, love others – 24/7. This is what we aim for.
We have plenty of parking, and there is easy access to the hall we meet in. The Sunday meeting begins at 10:30 am, but we serve coffee and cookies from 10:00-10.25 am. The refreshments are on us, sit wherever you like, and make yourself at home – we do.
We begin with about thirty minutes of singing where we express our love and thanks to God. Feel free to participate (the words are projected onto a screen) or simply listen. This is usually followed by a time of prayer and reflection as people to respond to God's Presence. Then there is an opportunity for people to give as part of their worship. Baskets of sweets are passed around and everyone takes one. Those who want to give a gift put it into the baskets as they are passed round.
Then the children leave for their program. The one exception to this is on the first week of each month when the kids program runs for the whole service. On these weeks they are signed in to their activities when you arrive.
Once the children are all signed in, there is a talk based on the Bible. It aims to apply the Bible to everyday life in a way that is challenging and inspiring. Following the talk, there is an opportunity for people to receive prayer. If this is new to you, feel free to watch – we will try to explain what is happening as we go along.
The service is over by 12:30 pm, but some people stay a while longer and chat. The children are brought back into the main hall for you to sign them out.

original text:
NOTE: This content is old - Published: Friday, Aug 2nd, 2013.
Children aged 7 and under must be accompanied by an adult. Charges apply for adults.
This drop in activity will get the creative juices flowing and give you the chance to design your own tile. Inspired by the designs of JC Edwards, Acrefair, design and paint your own tile. This event is suitable for under 16's.
Motorised fun in the pool and the perfect chance to race your friends. This event is suitable for under 16's.
Does what it says on the tin. This event is suitable for under 16's.
The Venture is a children's charity in Caia Park consisting of an adventure playground, early years centre, mentoring schemes and alternative learning programmes. They are celebrating their 35th birthday with a big pirates and princesses themed party. The event will see the official opening of a huge pirate ship built by the children and young people and made possible through a grant from WREN. All activities are free and food is also provided. Tiaras or eye patches are essential for entry.
This monthly event provides the perfect chance to wander around and do some shopping. There will be the chance to buy home made cakes, cards, craft, preserves, honey and paintings.
Test your balance and skill on this fun inflatable obstacle run. This event is suitable for under 16's.
Family Fun Day with stalls, children's rides and birds of prey, as well as reptiles, small animals and mini beasts from Reptile Kids. Free entry and free parking.
Indigo has two small performances. The toddler show uses puppets and characters, music and mayhem for a fun, interactive theatrical extravaganza. The Junior show uses masks and screen projections to create a tale about a young boy trying to be a knight. Both shows last 30-40 minutes. You don't need to book but there is a full capacity of 35 people for each show. This is event is suitable for 3-10year olds.
The chance to work out in the gym. This is event is suitable for Under 16's.
A guided woodland tour and the perfect way to spend a Sunday afternoon. Meet at the Ironworks. All are welcome.
Exercise in the water to funky music Mondays excluding bank holidays. This event is suitable for under 16's.
This weekly event is guaranteed to be a fun-filled morning. This is event is suitable for Under 16's.
A new way of teaching children the skills for a life in sport is set to give Welsh youngsters the same sporting start as in countries like Australia and New Zealand Dragon Multi-Skills and Sport will focus on embedding basic skills – such as agility, balance and co-ordination – that give the essential building blocks, before moving on to playing a specific sport. This event is suitable for ages 8 – 11.
Duck Race Day with lots of other fun activities to take part in. All are welcome.
Move gracefully in the water to the beat of the music. This event is suitable for under 16's.
Distance varies according to age group. Please register by calling 01978 292088. This event is suitable for ages 8 – 16.
NWMAT & LMHC would like any ex Mines Rescue Team members to pop along to Llay Miners Welfare at 2pm to chat about your time as Rescue Team members & also about the practices at the Mines Rescue Station. If you have any memorabilia, photo's etc from your time there we would be pleased to see it.
A 4 mile walk which starts near the Duke of Wellington Public House in Trevor, and links the new World Heritage Site at Pontcysyllte aqueduct with the surrounding countryside. Meet in the layby (LL14 3SG / Grid Ref. SJ 272 428). Unsurfaced paths, some steep inclines, some stiles. 4 miles / 2 hours. No dogs please.
Come inside the gallery as part of Wrexham's fabulous Play Day for some fun arty activities. This event is suitable for all ages.
Wrexham will once again be celebrating National Play Day on Queen's Square and Llwyn Isaf (the Guildhall field). People of all ages, from babies, children and young people to parents, professionals and grandparents, are invited to join in this free and fun event. This event is suitable for all ages.
Come along and design/decorate you funky flower pot frame using different craft materials. This event is suitable for all ages.
CV Workshops running 10am-1pm and then 6-9pm (max. of 10 people per group) with 1-2-1 sessions that can be booked between 2-5pm. Running every Wednesday from 3rd July. These workshops are fun, thought-provoking and provide practical steps to help you write an engaging CV, covering letter and find out about job opportunities.
The workshops are small, friendly and safe environments to help you gain confidence to get the job you want.
Come and join the qualified coaches and learn new skills and tactics. This event is suitable for under 16′s.
The chance to spend some quality time together and spend some time developing your swimming skills. This is event is suitable for 6months – 4 years. Advanced booking is required.
Learn to swim with masks and snorkels. This event is suitable for under 16′s.
The chance to let your imagination run wild and make your own clay creation. This event is suitable for all ages.
Grab some roller skates and have take part in this fun roller disco. This event is suitable for under 16′s.

original text:
At the risk of yelling "fire" in a crowded theater, we present a few parallels between recent action and the year leading up to the October 1987 crash.
The great mystery behind the trade-weighted dollar's nearly-10% YTD decline is that it's failed to fuel further gains (or any gains) in commodity prices in 2017.
The gap between crude oil prices and Energy sector RS is now much wider than seen even at that historic 2014 juncture. The "divergent" weakness in Energy stocks suggests that crude will likely trade lower.
Profitable investing overseas requires not one, but two, successful decisions: 1) select an outperforming asset class; and, 2) be in a currency that provides a favorable foreign exchange impact.
The market seemed hesitant to push the Trump trade any farther as new policies have focused on trade renegotiation and immigration, the less positive part of the policy package.
Can The Dollar Save Small Caps?
The dollar's moonshot in recent months has resuscitated a stock market leadership argument we haven't heard for a long time.
Xenophobia continues to be a handsomely rewarded trait for U.S.-based equity investors, with the MSCI World Ex USA Index down 3.8% YTD through December 3rd—and now (incredibly) unchanged from its May 2011 high. Comparable period gains for the S&P 500 are +12.2% YTD and +50% from spring 2011 highs.
Long before the U.S. dollar began to rebound, the current bull market in global stocks had already favored "provincial" portfolio managers focusing solely on U.S. stocks.
Are U.S. markets for labor and capital actually getting tight?
The recent strength in the dollar coincided with a spike in volatility and weakness in risky assets, but the relationship over the last couple years has been tenuous at best.
Overall demand slack, stubbornly low velocity of money, an overall stronger dollar, painfully low labor cost inflation and weakness in commodity prices are strong disinflationary forces.
Given global economic recovery, and Fed action meant to stimulate the U.S. economy, we expect that inflation fires will heat up significantly in 2011.
Steve presents the transcript of his April 3rd interview with Barron's Senior Editor, Sandra Ward.
We continue to be more optimistic about the dollar than most, and believe the post election U.S. dollar weakness was overdone.
For now the economic expansion remains healthy, but could fade some in the second half of the year.
We estimate +3.3% real GDP growth in 2005, after weakening in the second half of the year.
CPI and PPI declined more than expected in December due to impact of lower energy prices.
Continued U.S. dollar weakness could certainly be an inflation negative, but we are getting more optimistic about the dollar.
Higher oil prices and higher food prices pushed recent CPI and PPI higher. Next month's readings will likely show less inflation with oil prices coming down some recently.
In last month's "View From The North Country" Steve Leuthold outlined his case for a strong dollar. This issue, we just wanted to show the updated charts, and comment that the rally appears to have begun.
This month's "View From The North Country" presents data showing periods where interest rates (both long T-bonds and 90 day T-bills) rose and stocks also rose. It can happen!
Much of the upward price movement in commodity prices, as measured in U.S. dollars, is the result of the weak dollar. A weaker dollar has an inflationary impact on U.S. prices.
Do Small Caps Lead When The Dollar Is Falling?
Performance results make a case that a declining dollar may be more favorable to large caps than small caps.
Study shows market performance not too bad over six periods when dollar is declining.....Which groups also perform in such an environment?
The dithering dollar. Did we miss our golden opportunity? Some think there is current hope for Social Security reform. Is new NASDAQ rule an uptick or upchuck? The bears are howling.
Is Long Term U.S. Interest Rate History Really A Guide For Today?
U.S. corporations must pay almost 300 basis points more to borrow in U.S. dollars than in Swiss francs. Why is there such a difference?
The bond market is looking increasingly vulnerable. The economy appears to be deteriorating, inflation is nowhere in sight and short rates are declining. With all this going for it, why is the bond market stagnating?
War, recession, falling oil prices and other good inflation news combined to support the U.S. fixed income markets in January.
All things considered, the bond market held up well in July. And even though the dollar index slid about 4%, there was little progress on the deficit front and inflation fears rose with oil prices. These bond market negatives were offset by increasing evidence of a recession in the works.
After years of historical research and endless plotting of productivity trends, our constant vigilance and ceaseless monitoring of vital economic and industrial currents has revealed an amazing correlation. Herein Mr. Leuthold offers his sage wisdom and several solutions to domestic sagging productivity.

original text:
Cuffing season is coming to an end, but it's going out with a bang. Paris Hilton is officially engaged, and her ring is the size of all the diamond rings on your Instagram feed combined, plus some. Honestly, I don't know how she can even carry it around. Think of the largest engagement ring you've ever seen and then double it. That's probably not even close to the ring's size.
Odds are there have been a handful of engagements popping up on your newsfeed lately, but Hilton's is one for the books. The beauty creator slash DJ posted a photo of her recent Aspen-based mountaintop proposal to actor Chris Zylka on Instagram. Let's just say that people aren't looking at the scenery.
Out of the seven photos that Hilton posted on her timeline, your eyes will stop at one in particular — the one of her ring. She gave her fiancé a big kiss and wrapped her arm around him, which showed off the enormous ring. The massive pear-shaped diamond takes up half of her finger. Literally.
According to People, Hilton's engagement ring is worth $2 million and weighs in at around 20 carats.
"I said Yas!" Hilton captioned the photo. "So happy & excited to be engaged to the love of my life. My best friend & soulmate. Perfect for me in every way. So dedicated, loyal, loving & kindhearted. I feel like the luckiest girl in the world! You are my dream come true! Thank you for showing me that fairytales do exist."
This really does look like something out of a fairytale. Everything from the surroundings to the ring itself is absolutely unbelievable.
I can see why it jumped out to him.
Although the official ranking of the ring is 20 carats, Twitter has some other ways to describe how big the ring is. This ring is unlike anything that people have ever seen, and the reactions prove it.
I thought the old lady drops this diamond into the water at the end? Did Zylka go get it for her? If so, this is an even more amazing proposal, which I didn't think was possible.
This ring is the biggest and brightest that I've ever seen — no Kirakira app needed.
How is it possible that there are two rings this big floating around in the universe? The reason that I don't have a ring is that Cardi B and Paris Hilton used up all the diamonds.
Plot twist: This ring is fake. Just kidding, this is very much real, which is even crazier.
"It looks like a freaking ring pop."
Honestly, we should have all expected this.
If Hilton's left arm suddenly gets a lot more muscular, this is why.
This biggest joke of them all is that this isn't actually a joke at all.
But all jokes aside, congrats to Hilton on her engagement. If Hilton suddenly starts saying, "that's cold," this 20 carat ring is why.

Out of the seven photos that Hilton posted on her timeline, your eyes will stop at one in particular  -  the one of her ring. She gave her fiancé a big kiss and wrapped her arm around him, which showed off the enormous ring. The massive pear-shaped diamond takes up half of her finger. Literally.
According to People, Hilton's engagement ring is worth $2 million and weighs in at around 20 carats.
"I said Yas!" Hilton captioned the photo. "So happy & excited to be engaged to the love of my life. My best friend & soulmate. Perfect for me in every way. So dedicated, loyal, loving & kindhearted. I feel like the luckiest girl in the world! You are my dream come true! Thank you for showing me that fairytales do exist."
This really does look like something out of a fairytale. Everything from the surroundings to the ring itself is absolutely unbelievable.
I can see why it jumped out to him.
Although the official ranking of the ring is 20 carats, Twitter has some other ways to describe how big the ring is. This ring is unlike anything that people have ever seen, and the reactions prove it.
I thought the old lady drops this diamond into the water at the end? Did Zylka go get it for her? If so, this is an even more amazing proposal, which I didn't think was possible.
This ring is the biggest and brightest that I've ever seen  -  no Kirakira app needed.
How is it possible that there are two rings this big floating around in the universe? The reason that I don't have a ring is that Cardi B and Paris Hilton used up all the diamonds.
Plot twist: This ring is fake. Just kidding, this is very much real, which is even crazier.
"It looks like a freaking ring pop."
Honestly, we should have all expected this.
If Hilton's left arm suddenly gets a lot more muscular, this is why.
This biggest joke of them all is that this isn't actually a joke at all.
But all jokes aside, congrats to Hilton on her engagement. If Hilton suddenly starts saying, "that's cold," this 20 carat ring is why.

original text:
CAIRO SANGO, the Enfield Ignatians' winger, was part of the Herts U20S squad which has reached the Jason Leonard County Championship semi-finals in the wake of the 28-19 win over Cornwall at Cheshunt.
In a hard-fought contest, Herts led 21-12 at half-time and they made the game safe in the second period with a converted try to earn a home semi-final against Lancashire on April 14th.
A graduate of the Ignatians' youth system, Cairo Sango said: "The game went very well – [and] we came out very hard and strong, [but] we let things slip a little just before half-time.
Herts reached a similar stage last season, and their triumphant march to the last four began in the pool stages with wins over Sussex (24-19), Eastern Counties (43-20) and Kent (31-29).
